Title: Regulation of the alkaloid biosynthesis by miRNA in opium poppy

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred higher induction when pso-miR535 is low in capsule
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports alkaloid biosynthesis indirectly via jasmonic and linoleic acid production; miR535-mediated cleavage may reduce this support
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lox transcript was experimentally confirmed as a cleavage target of pso-miR535 by modified 5'-RLM-RACE. 2. LOX contributes to jasmonic and linoleic acid production, linked to alkaloid biosynthesis. 3. Prior work cited showed Lox induction caused sanguinarine accumulation, suggesting low miR535 in capsule may favor alkaloid production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Modified 5'-RLM-RACE; microarray expression analysis; literature-supported metabolic link
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LOX affects alkaloids indirectly through jasmonate/linoleate-related signaling or precursor processes rather than acting as a core BIA pathway enzyme.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRR1731699; miRNA microarray: GEO accession NA

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Target transcript higher in capsule whereas pso-miR159 is higher in stem
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No direct nutrient-metabolism effect established in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB101 was used as an example of a target transcript inversely correlated with pso-miR159. 2. It served as validation of miRNA-target negative correlation, not as a focal alkaloid gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included only as experimentally discussed target; not established as core alkaloid-metabolism regulator in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine at the 4'-O position to contribute to S-reticuline formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate leading to target alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal specialized metabolites including morphinan-related alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript level in stem than capsule in the qRT-PCR comparison discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to S-reticuline formation and thereby influences total benzylisoquinoline alkaloid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4-OMT was predicted as a target of pso-miR2161. 2. pso-miR2161 expression and 4-OMT transcript accumulation showed inverse tissue patterns. 3. The paper cites prior evidence that suppression of 4-OMT mRNA increased total alkaloid content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In silico target prediction; qRT-PCR inverse expression analysis with pso-miR2161
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; tissue comparison among root, stem, leaf and young capsule
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The effect on alkaloid content is discussed partly from prior literature rather than direct metabolite measurement in this paper; stem was highlighted for 4-OMT transcript accumulation due to phloem-related alkaloid biology.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for tuning BIA flux, but tissue-specific effects and prior contradictory suppression outcomes warrant caution.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRR1731699; miRNA microarray: GEO accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Methylated intermediate in morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 7-O-methylation in the conversion of S-reticuline toward morphinan alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate in morphinan alkaloid branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal specialized metabolites including morphinan alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lowest mRNA accumulation in capsule; higher in root than capsule
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Affects morphinan-related BIA biosynthesis; transcript repression is associated with altered BIA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7-OMT was predicted as a cleavage target of the novel miRNA pso-miR13. 2. pso-miR13 is expressed in all tissues and is higher in capsule than root. 3. 7-OMT transcript is more repressed in capsule, consistent with miRNA-mediated regulation. 4. Prior work cited indicates 7-OMT silencing caused BIA accumulation in latex.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In silico target prediction; qRT-PCR of pso-miR13 and 7-OMT transcript across tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct cleavage was predicted rather than experimentally RACE-confirmed in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for modifying morphinan alkaloid composition through tissue-specific regulatory engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRR1731699; miRNA microarray: GEO accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces codeinone to codeine in the morphinan branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor/product in morphinan branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Minor tissue differences reported for target gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially influences morphinan alkaloid output as a putative miRNA target
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COR transcript was predicted to be targeted by putative miRNA pso-miR t0047847. 2. qRT-PCR examined the putative miRNA and target transcript. 3. Only minor expression differences among tissues were observed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Putative miRNA assignment only; no direct cleavage validation reported.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to salutaridinol in morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Minor tissue differences reported for target gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially affects morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis as a putative miRNA-regulated step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAT transcript was predicted to be targeted by putative miRNA pso-miR t0013376. 2. qRT-PCR was used to examine the putative miRNA-target pair. 3. Tissue differences were minor.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Putative miRNA only; no direct cleavage validation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tyrosine/DOPA to provide amine precursors for BIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Minor tissue differences reported for target gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially influences BIA precursor supply as a putative miRNA target
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TYDC transcript was predicted to be targeted by putative miRNA pso-miR t0000199. 2. qRT-PCR tested the putative miRNA-target pair. 3. Only slight tissue differences were observed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted target relationship only in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAD-binding; berberine bridge enzyme domain-containing oxidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes S-reticuline to (S)-scoulerine in the BIA pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward downstream BIA branch products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely affects BIA branch flux; proposed as a possible miR408-regulated step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pso-miR408 was proposed to target an mRNA encoding an FAD-binding and BBE domain-containing reticuline oxidase-like protein. 2. This enzyme converts S-reticuline to (S)-scoulerine. 3. The paper suggests miR408 may regulate BIA biosynthesis through this step.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relationship is proposed in discussion; no direct target validation data shown here.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Present in all tested tissues; higher in capsule than root
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Novel miRNA proposed as a regulator of a key O-methyltransferase step in the morphinan branch
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely suppresses 7-OMT and thereby modulates BIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pso-miR13 is a novel opium poppy miRNA identified in this study. 2. It was predicted to target 7-OMT, a BIA-biosynthetic gene. 3. qRT-PCR confirmed pso-miR13 expression in all tissues and an inverse relationship with 7-OMT transcript, especially in capsule.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sRNA sequencing/direct cloning; target prediction; qRT-PCR of miRNA and target transcript
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct cleavage was not experimentally mapped by RLM-RACE in the paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRR1731699; miRNA microarray: GEO accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Compared in capsule and stem; inverse relationship with 4-OMT transcript
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Targets a central S-reticuline-forming step in BIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely modulates BIA biosynthesis through suppression of 4-OMT
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pso-miR2161 was identified and predicted to cleave 4-OMT transcript. 2. qRT-PCR showed higher pso-miR2161 expression in capsule than stem, while 4-OMT was higher in stem. 3. The study proposes pso-miR2161 as a BIA-regulatory miRNA.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directional cloning/sequence identification; target prediction; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion contains one apparent text inconsistency on tissue comparison, but the central conclusion is inverse miRNA-target expression.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: sRNA-seq: NCBI SRA SRR1731699; miRNA microarray: GEO accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FAD-binding and BBE domain-containing reticuline oxidase-like protein
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid branch conversion from S-reticuline to (S)-scoulerine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Proposed regulator of a branch-point oxidative step in BIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential regulator of BIA branch flux
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pso-miR408 was identified among conserved miRNAs. 2. Computational analyses suggested it targets a reticuline oxidase-like BBE-domain protein. 3. The paper proposes miR408 as a possible BIA-regulatory miRNA.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed only; no direct qRT-PCR or cleavage validation for this target pair in the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially modulates late morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Putative miRNA candidate pso-miR t0047847 was predicted from small RNA data. 2. It targets COR according to in silico analysis. 3. qRT-PCR assessed this putative miRNA and target transcript pair, showing only minor tissue differences.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA prediction; target prediction; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pre-miRNA sequence absent; only putative mature sequence-based inference.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential regulator of morphinan branch acetylation step
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Putative miRNA pso-miR t0013376 was predicted to target SAT. 2. The pair was further analysed by qRT-PCR. 3. Only minor tissue-specific differences were observed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA prediction; target prediction; qRT-PCR

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially modulates upstream precursor formation for BIA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Putative miRNA pso-miR t0000199 was predicted to target TYDC. 2. qRT-PCR tested the putative miRNA-target pair. 3. Minor tissue-specific differences were observed.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA prediction; target prediction; qRT-PCR
